highlights.text: [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status. [Subagent Task]: You are summarizing one internal OpenClaw chat transcript for durable memory retrieval. Return ONLY strict JSON with this schema: { "title": "string — short descriptive title for this chat", "summary": "string — 1-2 sentence factual summary", "projects": ["list of project names mentioned or worked on"], "tags": ["topic tags, 4-8 items"], "decisions": ["list of concrete decisions made"], "todos": ["outstanding tasks or next steps identified"], "user_preferences": ["durable preferences or requirements expressed by the user"], "followups": ["things promised or pending follow-up"], "importance": "low|medium|high" } Rules: - Be concise and factual. - Focus on decisions, durable context, project state, preferences, and follow-ups. - Ignore chatter and system boilerplate. - Do not include secrets, API keys, or credentials. - If information is absent, use empty arrays. - Output ONLY the JSON object, nothing else. Transcript to summarize: USER: Read HEARTBEAT.md if it exists (workspace context). Follow it strictly. Do not infer or repeat old tasks from prior chats. If nothing needs attention, reply HEARTBEAT_OK. When reading HEARTBEAT.md, use workspace file /Users/jl/.openclaw/workspace/HEARTBEAT.md (exact case). Do not read docs/heartbeat.md. Current time: Thursday, March 26th, 2026 — 4:17 AM (America/Los_Angeles) / 2026-03-26 11:17 UTC ASSISTANT: HEARTBEAT_OK USER: huh. had no idea each chat thread had a history and could be lost ASSISTANT: Explained that thread/session history can rotate or be deleted, so durable memory should live in workspace files like memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md and MEMORY.md. USER: yes, can we make a little process to save chat history in memory, and also check historical chat files when working on things? I've noticed context has been lost in the past. Let's automate this in a way that's very logical and allows future prompts and work to automaticlaly look over past conversations for context. Maybe saving a cheater file that indexes each chat memory so it's easy to quickly prompt that index and get to the right chat memories for context ASSISTANT: Added a chat memory system with CHAT_MEMORY_SYSTEM.md, memory/chat-index.md, scripts/session_memory.py, and updates to AGENTS.md / HEARTBEAT.md / MEMORY.md. Then extended it with auto-indexing, project-context lookup, heartbeat reminders, project-memory scaffolding, and tested isolated subagent summarization as the correct approach for higher-quality transcript summaries.

```json { "title": "Chat Memory System Implementation", "summary": "User discovered chat history can be lost between sessions and requested an automated system to preserve and index chat history.
